text: Large Language Models, or LLMs, are AI systems trained on vast amounts of text from the internet, books, and articles. They learn patterns in language, allowing them to predict and generate words based on a user's input. To get useful responses, people use prompt engineering—carefully wording questions or instructions to guide the model. These models can write stories, answer questions, and translate languages, making them powerful tools. However, they have limits: they don't truly understand meaning, sometimes give incorrect information, and can reflect biases in their training data. So, while impressive, they require careful use.
